core_mechanism: A gain-versus-loss criticality threshold in a diffusive (multiple-scattering) medium with distributed amplification: a single control parameter crosses a critical value above which random-walk feedback combined with local gain yields a self-sustained exponentially growing mode instead of decay — the same criticality condition as a diffusion-with-gain / neutron-multiplication chain reaction.
brief_reason: The abstract defines a single critical parameter (the on-resonance optical thickness b0, critical value ~300) that acts as a lasing threshold in a medium where the same elements provide both gain and scattering, i.e. a criticality condition where distributed amplification plus multiple-scattering feedback overtakes loss.
